mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5.7% indicates deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.7%, or the signal's IC drops below -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with declining inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals strong real economic activity and stable pricing, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce positive returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles, indicated by FEDFUNDS above 4.5%, compress equity valuations through higher discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the signal's IC is above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts (HOUST) and GDP (GDPC1) reflects robust demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to produce positive returns in > 40% of cases, or the joint effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent disinflation (CPIAUCSL < 2.5%) signals accommodative monetary policy, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL remains below 2.5%, or the signal's IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) coupled with declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a tightening labor market, boosting S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to produce positive returns in > 40% of cases, or the joint effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A housing boom (HOUST > 1066 thousand units) reflects strong demand for residential construction, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1066, or the signal's IC is below 0.03.
